87: // UnmarshalJSON implements a custom unmarshaler that handles the historical rename
88: // from the "plan_file" JSON key to "task_file", mirroring session.InstanceData.UnmarshalJSON.
89: func (r *instanceRecord) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
90: 	type Alias instanceRecord
91: 	aux := &struct {
92: 		*Alias
93: 		PlanFile string `json:"plan_file,omitempty"`
94: 	}{
95: 		Alias: (*Alias)(r),
96: 	}
97: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, aux); err != nil {
98: 		return err
99: 	}
100: 	if r.TaskFile == "" && aux.PlanFile != "" {
101: 		r.TaskFile = aux.PlanFile
102: 	}
103: 	return nil
104: }
105: 
106: // statusLabel converts an instanceStatus to a lowercase text label.
107: func statusLabel(s instanceStatus) string {
108: 	switch s {
109: 	case instanceRunning:
110: 		return "running"
111: 	case instanceReady:
112: 		return "ready"
113: 	case instanceLoading:
114: 		return "loading"
115: 	case instancePaused:
116: 		return "paused"
117: 	default:
118: 		return fmt.Sprintf("unknown(%d)", s)
119: 	}
120: }
121: 
122: // whiteSpaceRe matches one or more whitespace characters for session name sanitisation.
123: var whiteSpaceRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
124: 
125: // kasTmuxName converts a human-readable instance title to the kas_-prefixed tmux
126: // session name used by the session package.  It replicates toKasTmuxName from
127: // session/tmux without importing that package (which would create a cycle:
128: // session/tmux → cmd → session/tmux).
129: func kasTmuxName(title string) string {
130: 	name := whiteSpaceRe.ReplaceAllString(title, "")
131: 	name = strings.ReplaceAll(name, ".", "_")
132: 	return "kas_" + name
133: }
134: 
135: // buildResumeCommand reconstructs the tmux program command string for a resumed
136: // instance.  It mirrors the env-var and flag injection performed by TmuxSession.Start()
137: // so that the resumed agent is indistinguishable from a freshly started one.
138: func buildResumeCommand(rec instanceRecord, worktreePath string) string {
139: 	program := rec.Program
140: 
141: 	// Append --dangerously-skip-permissions for Claude if originally enabled.
142: 	if rec.SkipPermissions && strings.HasSuffix(program, "claude") {
143: 		program += " --dangerously-skip-permissions"
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	// Append --agent flag for typed roles (planner, coder, reviewer, fixer).
147: 	if rec.AgentType != "" && !strings.Contains(program, "--agent") {
148: 		program += " --agent " + rec.AgentType
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// Append opencode log redirection so debug logs are preserved.
152: 	// Use rec.Program (the unmodified base) for the suffix check, matching
153: 	// TmuxSession.Start() which uses t.program — the local `program` variable
154: 	// may already have --agent appended, changing the suffix.
155: 	if strings.HasSuffix(rec.Program, "opencode") {
156: 		logDir := filepath.Join(worktreePath, ".kasmos", "logs")
157: 		if err := os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755); err == nil {
158: 			logFile := filepath.Join(logDir, kasTmuxName(rec.Title)+".log")
159: 			program += " --print-logs 2>>'" + logFile + "'"
160: 		}
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	// Prepend KASMOS_MANAGED=1 so the agent knows it is managed by kasmos.
164: 	program = "KASMOS_MANAGED=1 " + program
165: 
166: 	// Prepend task identity env vars for parallel wave execution.
167: 	if rec.TaskNumber > 0 {
168: 		program = fmt.Sprintf("KASMOS_TASK=%d KASMOS_WAVE=%d KASMOS_PEERS=%d %s",
169: 			rec.TaskNumber, rec.WaveNumber, rec.PeerCount, program)
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	return program
173: }
174: 
175: // executeInstanceList reads raw InstancesData from state, optionally filters by
176: // status, and formats the result as a text table or JSON array.
177: //
178: // statusFilters is optional; when provided only instances whose status label
179: // matches any of the given values are included.
180: func executeInstanceList(state config.StateManager, format string, statusFilters ...string) string {
181: 	raw := state.GetInstances()
182: 
183: 	var records []instanceRecord
184: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &records); err != nil {
185: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v\n", err)
186: 	}
187: 
188: 	// Apply optional status filter.
189: 	if len(statusFilters) > 0 {
190: 		filterSet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(statusFilters))
191: 		for _, f := range statusFilters {
192: 			filterSet[strings.ToLower(f)] = struct{}{}
193: 		}
194: 		filtered := records[:0]
195: 		for _, r := range records {
196: 			if _, ok := filterSet[statusLabel(r.Status)]; ok {
197: 				filtered = append(filtered, r)
198: 			}
199: 		}
200: 		records = filtered
201: 	}
202: 
203: 	if len(records) == 0 {
204: 		if format == "json" {
205: 			return "[]\n"
206: 		}
207: 		return "no instances\n"
208: 	}
209: 
210: 	switch format {
211: 	case "json":
212: 		type jsonRecord struct {
213: 			Title     string `json:"title"`
214: 			Status    string `json:"status"`
215: 			Branch    string `json:"branch"`
216: 			Program   string `json:"program"`
217: 			TaskFile  string `json:"task_file,omitempty"`
218: 			AgentType string `json:"agent_type,omitempty"`
219: 			CreatedAt string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
220: 		}
221: 		out := make([]jsonRecord, 0, len(records))
222: 		for _, r := range records {
223: 			var createdAt string
224: 			if !r.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
225: 				createdAt = r.CreatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
226: 			}
227: 			out = append(out, jsonRecord{
228: 				Title:     r.Title,
229: 				Status:    statusLabel(r.Status),
230: 				Branch:    r.Branch,
231: 				Program:   r.Program,
232: 				TaskFile:  r.TaskFile,
233: 				AgentType: r.AgentType,
234: 				CreatedAt: createdAt,
235: 			})
236: 		}
237: 		data, err := json.Marshal(out)
238: 		if err != nil {
239: 			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v\n", err)
240: 		}
241: 		return string(data) + "\n"
242: 
243: 	default: // "text"
244: 		var sb strings.Builder
245: 		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(&sb, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
246: 		fmt.Fprintln(w, "TITLE\tSTATUS\tBRANCH\tPROGRAM\tTASK")
247: 		for _, r := range records {
248: 			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n",
249: 				r.Title,
250: 				statusLabel(r.Status),
251: 				r.Branch,
252: 				r.Program,
253: 				r.TaskFile,
254: 			)
255: 		}
256: 		w.Flush()
257: 		return sb.String()
258: 	}
259: }
260: 
261: // loadInstanceRecords reads and parses the raw instance JSON from state.
262: func loadInstanceRecords(state config.StateManager) ([]instanceRecord, error) {
263: 	raw := state.GetInstances()
264: 	var records []instanceRecord
265: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &records); err != nil {
266: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse instances: %w", err)
267: 	}
268: 	return records, nil
269: }
270: 
271: // findInstanceData finds an instance record by title. It first tries an exact
272: // match, then falls back to a substring match. Returns an error when no match
273: // is found or when the substring matches more than one record (ambiguous).
274: func findInstanceData(records []instanceRecord, title string) (instanceRecord, error) {
275: 	// Exact match takes precedence.
276: 	for _, r := range records {
277: 		if r.Title == title {
278: 			return r, nil
279: 		}
280: 	}
281: 
282: 	// Substring fallback.
283: 	var matches []instanceRecord
284: 	for _, r := range records {
285: 		if strings.Contains(r.Title, title) {
286: 			matches = append(matches, r)
287: 		}
288: 	}
289: 	switch len(matches) {
290: 	case 0:
291: 		return instanceRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("instance not found: %q", title)
292: 	case 1:
293: 		return matches[0], nil
294: 	default:
295: 		names := make([]string, len(matches))
296: 		for i, m := range matches {
297: 			names[i] = m.Title
298: 		}
299: 		return instanceRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("ambiguous instance %q matches: %s", title, strings.Join(names, ", "))
300: 	}
301: }
302: 
303: // validateStatusForAction checks whether the instance is in a state compatible
304: // with the requested action and returns an error when it is not.
305: //
306: //   - kill:   allowed in any status
307: //   - pause:  not allowed when already paused
308: //   - resume: only allowed when paused
309: //   - send:   not allowed when paused
310: func validateStatusForAction(data instanceRecord, action string) error {
311: 	switch action {
312: 	case "kill":
313: 		// kill is allowed in any status
314: 		return nil
315: 	case "pause":
316: 		if data.Status == instancePaused {
317: 			return fmt.Errorf("instance is already paused")
318: 		}
319: 		return nil
320: 	case "resume":
321: 		if data.Status != instancePaused {
322: 			return fmt.Errorf("can only resume paused instances (current status: %s)", statusLabel(data.Status))
323: 		}
324: 		return nil
325: 	case "send":
326: 		if data.Status == instancePaused {
327: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot send prompt to a paused instance")
328: 		}
329: 		return nil
330: 	default:
331: 		return fmt.Errorf("unknown action: %q", action)
332: 	}
333: }
334: 
335: // removeInstanceFromState removes the named instance from persisted state.
336: // All remaining instance records are preserved verbatim — no fields are dropped.
337: func removeInstanceFromState(state config.StateManager, title string) error {
338: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
339: 	if err != nil {
340: 		return err
341: 	}
342: 	remaining := records[:0]
343: 	for _, r := range records {
344: 		if r.Title != title {
345: 			remaining = append(remaining, r)
346: 		}
347: 	}
348: 	raw, err := json.Marshal(remaining)
349: 	if err != nil {
350: 		return fmt.Errorf("marshal instances: %w", err)
351: 	}
352: 	return state.SaveInstances(raw)
353: }
354: 
355: // updateInstanceInState finds the named instance, applies updater to a copy,
356: // and persists the modified list back to state.
357: // All fields of every record (including unmodified ones) are preserved verbatim.
358: func updateInstanceInState(state config.StateManager, title string, updater func(*instanceRecord) error) error {
359: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
360: 	if err != nil {
361: 		return err
362: 	}
363: 	found := false
364: 	for i := range records {
365: 		if records[i].Title == title {
366: 			if err := updater(&records[i]); err != nil {
367: 				return err
368: 			}
369: 			found = true
370: 			break
371: 		}
372: 	}
373: 	if !found {
374: 		return fmt.Errorf("instance not found: %q", title)
375: 	}
376: 	raw, err := json.Marshal(records)
377: 	if err != nil {
378: 		return fmt.Errorf("marshal instances: %w", err)
379: 	}
380: 	return state.SaveInstances(raw)
381: }
382: 
383: // instanceStatusSummary holds counts of instances grouped by their status bucket.
384: type instanceStatusSummary struct {
385: 	Running int
386: 	Ready   int
387: 	Paused  int
388: 	Killed  int
389: }
390: 
391: // summarizeInstanceStatus aggregates instance records into status buckets.
392: // instanceRunning and instanceLoading both increment Running (matches app semantics).
393: // Any unrecognised status value goes to Killed as a future-proof bucket.
394: func summarizeInstanceStatus(records []instanceRecord) instanceStatusSummary {
395: 	var s instanceStatusSummary
396: 	for _, r := range records {
397: 		switch r.Status {
398: 		case instanceRunning, instanceLoading:
399: 			s.Running++
400: 		case instanceReady:
401: 			s.Ready++
402: 		case instancePaused:
403: 			s.Paused++
404: 		default:
405: 			s.Killed++
406: 		}
407: 	}
408: 	return s
409: }
410: 
411: // executeInstanceStatus loads all instance records from state and returns a
412: // tabwriter-formatted summary table as a string. Returns "no instances found\n"
413: // when the state contains no records.
414: func executeInstanceStatus(state config.StateManager) (string, error) {
415: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
416: 	if err != nil {
417: 		return "", err
418: 	}
419: 	if len(records) == 0 {
420: 		return "no instances found\n", nil
421: 	}
422: 	summary := summarizeInstanceStatus(records)
423: 	var sb strings.Builder
424: 	w := tabwriter.NewWriter(&sb, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
425: 	fmt.Fprintln(w, "STATE\tCOUNT")
426: 	fmt.Fprintf(w, "running\t%d\n", summary.Running)
427: 	fmt.Fprintf(w, "ready\t%d\n", summary.Ready)
428: 	fmt.Fprintf(w, "paused\t%d\n", summary.Paused)
429: 	fmt.Fprintf(w, "killed\t%d\n", summary.Killed)
430: 	w.Flush()
431: 	return sb.String(), nil
432: }
433: 
434: // NewInstanceCmd builds the `kas instance` cobra command tree.
435: func NewInstanceCmd() *cobra.Command {
436: 	instanceCmd := &cobra.Command{
437: 		Use:   "instance",
438: 		Short: "manage agent instances (list, kill, pause, resume, send)",
439: 	}
440: 
441: 	// kas instance list
442: 	var format string
443: 	var statusFilter string
444: 	listCmd := &cobra.Command{
445: 		Use:   "list",
446: 		Short: "list all agent instances",
447: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
448: 			state := config.LoadState()
449: 			var filters []string
450: 			if statusFilter != "" {
451: 				filters = append(filters, statusFilter)
452: 			}
453: 			fmt.Print(executeInstanceList(state, format, filters...))
454: 			return nil
455: 		},
456: 	}
457: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&format, "format", "text", "output format: text or json")
458: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&statusFilter, "status", "", "filter by status (running, ready, loading, paused)")
459: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(listCmd)
460: 
461: 	// kas instance kill <title>
462: 	killCmd := &cobra.Command{
463: 		Use:   "kill <title>",
464: 		Short: "pause an agent instance and preserve its branch (safe kill)",
465: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
466: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
467: 			title := args[0]
468: 			state := config.LoadState()
469: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
470: 			if err != nil {
471: 				return err
472: 			}
473: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
474: 			if err != nil {
475: 				return err
476: 			}
477: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "kill"); err != nil {
478: 				return err
479: 			}
480: 			// Auto-commit dirty changes before removing worktree.
481: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" {
482: 				commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] auto-save from '%s' on %s (killed)",
483: 					rec.Title, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
484: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "add", "-A").Run()
485: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath,
486: 					"commit", "-m", commitMsg, "--allow-empty").Run()
487: 			}
488: 			// Kill tmux session (best-effort).
489: 			_ = exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title)).Run()
490: 			// Remove worktree but preserve branch.
491: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" && rec.Worktree.RepoPath != "" {
492: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath,
493: 					"worktree", "remove", "--force", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath).Run()
494: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath,
495: 					"worktree", "prune").Run()
496: 			}
497: 			// Set to paused instead of removing from state.
498: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
499: 				r.Status = instancePaused
500: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
501: 				return nil
502: 			}); err != nil {
503: 				return err
504: 			}
505: 			fmt.Printf("paused: %s (branch preserved)\n", rec.Title)
506: 			return nil
507: 		},
508: 	}
509: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(killCmd)
510: 
511: 	// kas instance pause <title>
512: 	pauseCmd := &cobra.Command{
513: 		Use:   "pause <title>",
514: 		Short: "pause an agent instance (saves branch, removes worktree)",
515: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
516: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
517: 			title := args[0]
518: 			state := config.LoadState()
519: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
520: 			if err != nil {
521: 				return err
522: 			}
523: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
524: 			if err != nil {
525: 				return err
526: 			}
527: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "pause"); err != nil {
528: 				return err
529: 			}
530: 			// Commit any dirty changes in the worktree before pausing.
531: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" {
532: 				commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] update from '%s' on %s (paused)", rec.Title, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
533: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "add", "-A").Run()
534: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "commit", "-m", commitMsg, "--allow-empty").Run()
535: 			}
536: 			// Kill the tmux session using the kas_-prefixed name.
537: 			_ = exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title)).Run()
538: 			// Remove the worktree (preserve the branch).
539: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" && rec.Worktree.RepoPath != "" {
540: 				if err := exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "remove", "--force", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath).Run(); err != nil {
541: 					return fmt.Errorf("remove worktree: %w", err)
542: 				}
543: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "prune").Run()
544: 			}
545: 			// Update state: mark as paused and clear the worktree path.
546: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
547: 				r.Status = instancePaused
548: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
549: 				return nil
550: 			}); err != nil {
551: 				return err
552: 			}
553: 			fmt.Printf("paused: %s\n", rec.Title)
554: 			return nil
555: 		},
556: 	}
557: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(pauseCmd)
558: 
559: 	// kas instance resume <title>
560: 	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
561: 		Use:   "resume <title>",
562: 		Short: "resume a paused agent instance",
563: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
564: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
565: 			title := args[0]
566: 			state := config.LoadState()
567: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
568: 			if err != nil {
569: 				return err
570: 			}
571: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
572: 			if err != nil {
573: 				return err
574: 			}
575: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "resume"); err != nil {
576: 				return err
577: 			}
578: 			if rec.Worktree.RepoPath == "" || rec.Worktree.BranchName == "" {
579: 				return fmt.Errorf("instance %q has no stored worktree metadata; cannot resume", rec.Title)
580: 			}
581: 			// Derive the worktree path from the repo path and session name.
582: 			worktreePath := rec.Worktree.WorktreePath
583: 			if worktreePath == "" {
584: 				// Reconstruct using the conventional path: <worktree-root>/<title>
585: 				worktreePath = rec.Path
586: 			}
587: 			// Re-add the git worktree on the preserved branch.
588: 			if err := exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "add", worktreePath, rec.Worktree.BranchName).Run(); err != nil {
589: 				return fmt.Errorf("recreate worktree: %w", err)
590: 			}
591: 			// Reconstruct the full program command (env vars + flags) matching the original session.
592: 			program := buildResumeCommand(rec, worktreePath)
593: 			// Start a new tmux session using the kas_-prefixed name.
594: 			sessionName := kasTmuxName(rec.Title)
595: 			if err := exec.Command("tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", sessionName, "-c", worktreePath, program).Run(); err != nil {
596: 				return fmt.Errorf("start tmux session: %w", err)
597: 			}
598: 			// Update state: mark as running and store the restored worktree path.
599: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
600: 				r.Status = instanceRunning
601: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = worktreePath
602: 				return nil
603: 			}); err != nil {
604: 				return err
605: 			}
606: 			fmt.Printf("resumed: %s\n", rec.Title)
607: 			return nil
608: 		},
609: 	}
610: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(resumeCmd)
611: 
612: 	// kas instance send <title> <prompt>
613: 	sendCmd := &cobra.Command{
614: 		Use:   "send <title> <prompt>",
615: 		Short: "send a prompt to an agent instance",
616: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(2),
617: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
618: 			title := args[0]
619: 			prompt := args[1]
620: 			state := config.LoadState()
621: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
622: 			if err != nil {
623: 				return err
624: 			}
625: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
626: 			if err != nil {
627: 				return err
628: 			}
629: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "send"); err != nil {
630: 				return err
631: 			}
632: 			// Use kas_-prefixed session name to match how the session was created.
633: 			if err := exec.Command("tmux", "send-keys", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title), prompt, "Enter").Run(); err != nil {
634: 				return fmt.Errorf("send keys: %w", err)
635: 			}
636: 			fmt.Printf("sent to %s\n", rec.Title)
637: 			return nil
638: 		},
639: 	}
640: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(sendCmd)
641: 
642: 	// kas instance status
643: 	statusCmd := &cobra.Command{
644: 		Use:   "status",
645: 		Short: "show status summary for all instances",
646: 		Args:  cobra.NoArgs,
647: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
648: 			out, err := executeInstanceStatus(config.LoadState())
649: 			if err != nil {
650: 				return err
651: 			}
652: 			fmt.Print(out)
653: 			return nil
654: 		},
655: 	}
656: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(statusCmd)
657: 
658: 	return instanceCmd
659: }

42: 	s := config.DefaultState()
43: 	s.InstancesData = raw
44: 	return s
45: }

164: // fullInstanceRecord returns an instanceRecord with every field set to a
165: // non-zero/non-default value.  Used by round-trip tests to verify that
166: // removeInstanceFromState and updateInstanceInState do not silently drop any
167: // field when they deserialise and re-serialise the state JSON.
168: func fullInstanceRecord(title string) instanceRecord {
169: 	return instanceRecord{
170: 		Title:                  title,
171: 		Path:                   "/worktrees/" + title,
172: 		Branch:                 "feat/" + title,
173: 		Status:                 instanceRunning,
174: 		Height:                 48,
175: 		Width:                  220,
176: 		CreatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
177: 		UpdatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
178: 		Program:                "claude",
179: 		AutoYes:                true,
180: 		SkipPermissions:        true,
181: 		TaskFile:               title + ".md",
182: 		AgentType:              "coder",
183: 		TaskNumber:             3,
184: 		WaveNumber:             2,
185: 		PeerCount:              4,
186: 		IsReviewer:             false,
187: 		ImplementationComplete: true,
188: 		SoloAgent:              false,
189: 		QueuedPrompt:           "hello from " + title,
190: 		ReviewCycle:            2,
191: 		Worktree: instanceWorktree{
192: 			RepoPath:      "/repo",
193: 			WorktreePath:  "/repo/.worktrees/" + title,
194: 			SessionName:   title,
195: 			BranchName:    "feat/" + title,
196: 			BaseCommitSHA: "deadbeef" + title,
197: 		},
198: 		DiffStats: instanceDiffStats{
199: 			Added:   10,
200: 			Removed: 5,
201: 			Content: "some diff for " + title,
202: 		},
203: 	}
204: }

206: // assertRecordFieldsEqual asserts that all fields of want and got are equal.
207: // This is the canonical checker used by round-trip tests.
208: func assertRecordFieldsEqual(t *testing.T, want, got instanceRecord) {
209: 	t.Helper()
210: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Title, got.Title, "Title")
211: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Path, got.Path, "Path")
212: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Branch, got.Branch, "Branch")
213: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Status, got.Status, "Status")
214: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Height, got.Height, "Height")
215: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Width, got.Width, "Width")
216: 	assert.True(t, want.CreatedAt.Equal(got.CreatedAt), "CreatedAt: want %v got %v", want.CreatedAt, got.CreatedAt)
217: 	assert.True(t, want.UpdatedAt.Equal(got.UpdatedAt), "UpdatedAt: want %v got %v", want.UpdatedAt, got.UpdatedAt)
218: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Program, got.Program, "Program")
219: 	assert.Equal(t, want.AutoYes, got.AutoYes, "AutoYes")
220: 	assert.Equal(t, want.SkipPermissions, got.SkipPermissions, "SkipPermissions")
221: 	assert.Equal(t, want.TaskFile, got.TaskFile, "TaskFile")
222: 	assert.Equal(t, want.AgentType, got.AgentType, "AgentType")
223: 	assert.Equal(t, want.TaskNumber, got.TaskNumber, "TaskNumber")
224: 	assert.Equal(t, want.WaveNumber, got.WaveNumber, "WaveNumber")
225: 	assert.Equal(t, want.PeerCount, got.PeerCount, "PeerCount")
226: 	assert.Equal(t, want.IsReviewer, got.IsReviewer, "IsReviewer")
227: 	assert.Equal(t, want.ImplementationComplete, got.ImplementationComplete, "ImplementationComplete")
228: 	assert.Equal(t, want.SoloAgent, got.SoloAgent, "SoloAgent")
229: 	assert.Equal(t, want.QueuedPrompt, got.QueuedPrompt, "QueuedPrompt")
230: 	assert.Equal(t, want.ReviewCycle, got.ReviewCycle, "ReviewCycle")
231: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Worktree.RepoPath, got.Worktree.RepoPath, "Worktree.RepoPath")
232: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Worktree.WorktreePath, got.Worktree.WorktreePath, "Worktree.WorktreePath")
233: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Worktree.SessionName, got.Worktree.SessionName, "Worktree.SessionName")
234: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Worktree.BranchName, got.Worktree.BranchName, "Worktree.BranchName")
235: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, got.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, "Worktree.BaseCommitSHA")
236: 	assert.Equal(t, want.DiffStats.Added, got.DiffStats.Added, "DiffStats.Added")
237: 	assert.Equal(t, want.DiffStats.Removed, got.DiffStats.Removed, "DiffStats.Removed")
238: 	assert.Equal(t, want.DiffStats.Content, got.DiffStats.Content, "DiffStats.Content")
239: }

241: // TestRemoveInstanceFromState_PreservesFields verifies that removing one instance
242: // does not corrupt any fields of the remaining instances during the JSON round-trip.
243: // This is the regression test for the data-loss bug where instanceRecord was
244: // incomplete and silently dropped ~15 fields from every surviving instance.
245: func TestRemoveInstanceFromState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {
246: 	keeper := fullInstanceRecord("keeper")
247: 	goner := fullInstanceRecord("goner")
248: 
249: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{keeper, goner})
250: 
251: 	err := removeInstanceFromState(state, "goner")
252: 	require.NoError(t, err)
253: 
254: 	remaining, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
255: 	require.NoError(t, err)
256: 	require.Len(t, remaining, 1, "expected exactly one instance to remain after removal")
257: 
258: 	assertRecordFieldsEqual(t, keeper, remaining[0])
259: }

280: // TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields verifies that updating one field of one
281: // instance does not corrupt any fields of the other instances during the JSON
282: // round-trip.  This is the regression test for the data-loss bug in pause/resume.
283: func TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {
284: 	untouched := fullInstanceRecord("untouched")
285: 	target := fullInstanceRecord("target")
286: 	target.Status = instancePaused // start paused so we can "resume" it
287: 
288: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{untouched, target})
289: 
290: 	// Simulate what pause does: sets status to paused and clears the worktree path.
291: 	err := updateInstanceInState(state, "target", func(r *instanceRecord) error {
292: 		r.Status = instancePaused
293: 		r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
294: 		return nil
295: 	})
296: 	require.NoError(t, err)
297: 
298: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
299: 	require.NoError(t, err)
300: 	require.Len(t, records, 2)
301: 
302: 	// untouched instance must have ALL its fields preserved.
303: 	var gotUntouched, gotTarget instanceRecord
304: 	for _, r := range records {
305: 		switch r.Title {
306: 		case "untouched":
307: 			gotUntouched = r
308: 		case "target":
309: 			gotTarget = r
310: 		}
311: 	}
312: 
313: 	assertRecordFieldsEqual(t, untouched, gotUntouched)
314: 
315: 	// target must have only Status and Worktree.WorktreePath changed.
316: 	assert.Equal(t, instancePaused, gotTarget.Status, "target.Status should be paused")
317: 	assert.Empty(t, gotTarget.Worktree.WorktreePath, "target.Worktree.WorktreePath should be cleared")
318: 	// All other fields of target should be preserved.
319: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Height, gotTarget.Height, "target.Height")
320: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Width, gotTarget.Width, "target.Width")
321: 	assert.Equal(t, target.AutoYes, gotTarget.AutoYes, "target.AutoYes")
322: 	assert.Equal(t, target.SkipPermissions, gotTarget.SkipPermissions, "target.SkipPermissions")
323: 	assert.Equal(t, target.TaskNumber, gotTarget.TaskNumber, "target.TaskNumber")
324: 	assert.Equal(t, target.WaveNumber, gotTarget.WaveNumber, "target.WaveNumber")
325: 	assert.Equal(t, target.PeerCount, gotTarget.PeerCount, "target.PeerCount")
326: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ImplementationComplete, gotTarget.ImplementationComplete, "target.ImplementationComplete")
327: 	assert.Equal(t, target.QueuedPrompt, gotTarget.QueuedPrompt, "target.QueuedPrompt")
328: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ReviewCycle, gotTarget.ReviewCycle, "target.ReviewCycle")
329: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.RepoPath, gotTarget.Worktree.RepoPath, "target.Worktree.RepoPath")
330: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.SessionName, gotTarget.Worktree.SessionName, "target.Worktree.SessionName")
331: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BranchName, gotTarget.Worktree.BranchName, "target.Worktree.BranchName")
332: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, gotTarget.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, "target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA")
333: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Added, gotTarget.DiffStats.Added, "target.DiffStats.Added")
334: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Removed, gotTarget.DiffStats.Removed, "target.DiffStats.Removed")
335: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Content, gotTarget.DiffStats.Content, "target.DiffStats.Content")
336: }

387: // TestKillCmd_SetsInstanceToPaused verifies that the kill command updates the
388: // instance to paused status instead of removing it from state.
389: func TestKillCmd_SetsInstanceToPaused(t *testing.T) {
390: 	rec := fullInstanceRecord("kill-target")
391: 	rec.Status = instanceRunning
392: 	other := fullInstanceRecord("other")
393: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{rec, other})
394: 
395: 	// Simulate what the new kill logic should do: update to paused, not remove.
396: 	err := updateInstanceInState(state, "kill-target", func(r *instanceRecord) error {
397: 		r.Status = instancePaused
398: 		r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
399: 		return nil
400: 	})
401: 	require.NoError(t, err)
402: 
403: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
404: 	require.NoError(t, err)
405: 	require.Len(t, records, 2, "kill should NOT remove instance from state")
406: 
407: 	var target instanceRecord
408: 	for _, r := range records {
409: 		if r.Title == "kill-target" {
410: 			target = r
411: 		}
412: 	}
413: 	assert.Equal(t, instancePaused, target.Status, "killed instance should be paused")
414: 	assert.Empty(t, target.Worktree.WorktreePath, "worktree path should be cleared")
415: }

482: // TestSummarizeInstanceStatus_Mixed verifies aggregation across all known and
483: // unknown status values including instanceLoading (which counts as running).

507: // TestExecuteInstanceStatus_OutputOrder verifies deterministic row order in the
508: // tabwriter output: running, ready, paused, killed.
509: func TestExecuteInstanceStatus_OutputOrder(t *testing.T) {
510: 	state := newTestStateFromRaw(t, []instanceTestData{
511: 		{Title: "a", Status: 3 /* Paused */},
512: 		{Title: "b", Status: 0 /* Running */},
513: 		{Title: "c", Status: 1 /* Ready */},
514: 	})
515: 	out, err := executeInstanceStatus(state)
516: 	require.NoError(t, err)
517: 
518: 	// Verify header and row presence
519: 	assert.Contains(t, out, "STATE")
520: 	assert.Contains(t, out, "COUNT")
521: 
522: 	// Verify deterministic order: running before ready before paused before killed
523: 	runIdx := strings.Index(out, "running")
524: 	readyIdx := strings.Index(out, "ready")
525: 	pausedIdx := strings.Index(out, "paused")
526: 	killedIdx := strings.Index(out, "killed")
527: 	assert.Greater(t, readyIdx, runIdx, "ready should come after running")
528: 	assert.Greater(t, pausedIdx, readyIdx, "paused should come after ready")
529: 	assert.Greater(t, killedIdx, pausedIdx, "killed should come after paused")
530: }

533: // returns an error containing "parse instances".
534: func TestExecuteInstanceStatus_ParseError(t *testing.T) {
535: 	s := config.DefaultState()
536: 	s.InstancesData = []byte(`not-valid-json`)
537: 	_, err := executeInstanceStatus(s)

561: 	records := []instanceRecord{{Title: "stale", Status: instanceStatus(99)}}
562: 	summary := summarizeInstanceStatus(records)

564: 	assert.Equal(t, 0, summary.Running)
565: 	assert.Equal(t, 0, summary.Ready)

567: }

28: // GetConfigDir returns the XDG-compliant config directory (~/.config/kasmos/).
29: // On first call without that directory, it attempts to migrate legacy directories
30: // (.klique, then .hivemind) via rename. If migration fails it returns the old path.
31: func GetConfigDir() (string, error) {
32: 	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()

46: 		filepath.Join(home, ".klique"),
47: 		filepath.Join(home, ".hivemind"),
48: 	} {
49: 		if _, statErr := os.Stat(legacy); statErr != nil {
50: 			continue
51: 		}
52: 		// Ensure ~/.config/ parent exists before rename.
53: 		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0755); mkErr != nil {
54: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to create %s: %v", filepath.Dir(target), mkErr)
55: 			return legacy, nil
56: 		}
57: 		if mvErr := os.Rename(legacy, target); mvErr != nil {
58: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to migrate %s to %s: %v", legacy, target, mvErr)
59: 			return legacy, nil
60: 		}
61: 		return target, nil
62: 	}
63:

65: 	return target, nil
66: }

22: // (~/.config/kasmos/) and appends "taskstore.db". This path is shared with
23: // the auditlog SQLiteLogger so both can coexist in the same database file
24: // (each using a separate table).

26: 	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
27: 	if err != nil {
28: 		return filepath.Join(".", "taskstore.db")
29: 	}

parts.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the implementation. Let me compile my review findings.

## Review Summary

### Spec Compliance Check

**Task 1: `kas audit` subcommand** — ✅ Complete
- `NewAuditCmd()`, `executeAuditList()`, `openAuditLogger()` all implemented per spec
- `formatAuditDetails()` and `renderAuditRows()` helper functions present
- Tabwriter output with TIME/EVENT/DETAILS columns
- Empty-state: `"no audit entries found\n"` — correct
- Limit validation, event filtering, error wrapping all correct
- Root wiring in `cmd/cmd.go` — present
- Tests: `TestFormatAuditDetails`, `TestRenderAuditRows_HeaderAndOrder`, `TestAuditList_FormatsTable`, `TestAuditList_Empty`, `TestAuditList_EventFilter`, `TestAuditCmd_RejectsNonPositiveLimit`, `TestAuditCmd_Wiring` — all present

**Task 2: `kas tmux` subcommand group** — ✅ Complete
- `NewTmuxCmd()` with `list`, `adopt`, `kill` subcommands
- `discoverKasSessions()`, `executeTmuxList()`, `executeTmuxAdopt()`, `executeTmuxKill()` all implemented
- `relativeAge()` helper, `buildKnownNames()` helper
- Orphan filtering, validation order, error messages all match spec
- State round-trip preservation verified
- Root wiring in `cmd/cmd.go` — present
- Comprehensive test coverage with mock executor

**Task 3: `kas instance status` subcommand** — ✅ Complete
- `instanceStatusSummary` struct, `summarizeInstanceStatus()`, `executeInstanceStatus()` all implemented
- Status buckets: running (includes loading), ready, paused, killed (default) — matches app semantics
- Tabwriter output with STATE/COUNT columns in fixed order
- Empty-state: `"no instances found\n"` — correct
- Tests cover mixed statuses, empty state, output order, parse errors, unknown status, subcommand wiring

### Out-of-Scope Changes

The branch includes a **config directory rewrite** (reverting `GetConfigDir()` from project-local `.kasmos/` back to XDG `~/.config/kasmos/`) and related changes to `config/taskstore/factory.go`, `config/config_test.go`, `config/toml.go`, `internal/initcmd/`, `main.go`, `.gitignore`, and deletion of `docs/plans/move-default-config.md`. These are from commit `66dccd28` ("fix: recover from dead elaborators and accept flexible task header separators"). This is scope creep relative to the cli-monitoring plan, but since it's in a separate commit and all tests pass, it's not blocking.

### Issues Found

Let me look at one specific issue more carefully:

I've identified the issues. The table-driven loop at lines 116-126 is a no-op test — it asserts `true` unconditionally. The condition `if tc.limit <= 0` is always true for both test cases (0 and -5), and `assert.True(t, true, ...)` always passes. This is dead code that tests nothing. However, the second part of the test (lines 128-138) does properly test the limit validation through cobra. The table-driven part should be removed since it provides no value.

The `audit list` subcommand is missing a `Short` description. Every other subcommand has one. This is a minor issue I can self-fix.

Now let me also verify the `TestAuditCmd_RejectsNonPositiveLimit` negative-limit case is actually tested. The second part only tests limit=0:

Looking at lines 128-138, the cobra test only tests `limit=0`. The `limit=-5` case is only "tested" in the no-op table-driven loop. However, the actual validation logic in `RunE` is `if limit <= 0`, which covers both cases. The cobra test with limit=0 is sufficient to verify the guard works. The table-driven loop is still dead code though.
